steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says small white herons prefer extensive canopy cover, but glossy ibises do not share that preference. The blank completes a conclusion: canopy-increasing strategies are less likely to ____. The correct completion must follow directly from the data.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'attract glossy ibises to the area than they are to attract small white herons to the area.': Since glossy ibises don't prefer canopy cover but small white herons do, a canopy-increasing strategy would benefit herons more than ibises — so it is less likely to attract ibises. This follows directly from the passage. ✓
  • Choice B — 'extend the average lifespan of small white herons already in the area than they are to attract a greater number of those birds to the area.': The passage compares the two species, not lifespan vs. attraction for one species. This distinction is not in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — 'decrease the area's appeal to both small white herons and glossy ibises than they are to increase the appeal to other species.': The passage says herons prefer canopy cover, so a canopy-increasing strategy would increase appeal to herons — not decrease it. This contradicts the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — 'attract birds that don't typically live in wetlands to the area than they are to attract small white herons or glossy ibises to the area.': Non-wetland birds are never mentioned. This introduces information outside the passage. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
